mission.title: Install and Configure Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE)
mission.description: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications using Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who want to leverage Kubernetes without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on Google Cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Google Cloud SDK
steps.description:
This step installs the Google Cloud SDK, which includes the gcloud command-line tool needed to interact with GKE.

```bash

curl https://sdk.cloud.google.com | bash

exec -l $SHELL

```

> Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the installation.
> Reload the shell to make gcloud available.

steps.title: Create a GKE Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new GKE cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node configuration.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'my-gke-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures kubectl to use the credentials for the newly created GKE cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure the cluster name matches the one you created.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up basic monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-autoscaling --min-nodes=1 --max-nodes=5 --region=us-central1

gcloud container clusters update my-gke-cluster --enable-cloud-logging --enable-cloud-monitoring --region=us-central1

```

> This enables autoscaling for the node pool.
> This enables logging and monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the GKE Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources, which may result in data loss.

```bash

gcloud container clusters delete my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This will remove the cluster and all resources associated with it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the GKE Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your workloads. This command upgrades the cluster to the latest version.

```bash

gcloud container clusters upgrade my-gke-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --region=us-central1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace '1.31' with the desired version.
> Verify that the nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x.x.x.x'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the cluster is not running or kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials my-gke-cluster --region=us-central1' to set up kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the image is not available in the specified container registry.

**Fix:**
Ensure the image exists and is accessible from the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to resource constraints or issues with the node itself.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' to identify the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new GKE Autopilot features, improved workload identity management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure your workloads are compatible with the new features before upgrading.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Google Cloud SDK installed and configured.
